name: ChargePoint EV Charging API
what_it_does: EV charging network API for accessing ChargePoint's 115,000+ charging stations — querying station availability, initiating charging sessions, monitoring usage, and managing fleet charging.
use_cases: Querying nearby EV charging station availability for route planning agents
Starting and stopping charging sessions programmatically for fleet management
Monitoring charging session status and energy delivered for fleet cost tracking
Managing charging schedules and access control for corporate EV fleet programs
Integrating ChargePoint data with fleet management systems for EV fleet automation
not_for: Consumer apps without ChargePoint partner agreement
Non-North American deployments (ChargePoint is primarily US/Canada)
Building custom charging hardware (use OCPP protocol directly)
best_when: An agent manages corporate EV fleet charging or needs to integrate ChargePoint station data into fleet management or energy management workflows.
avoid_when: You need multi-network EV charging data — ChargePoint API only covers ChargePoint stations.

auth.notes: OAuth2 for user authorization. API key required for application registration (partner program). Access requires ChargePoint developer account approval. User tokens for station access and session management.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier; development access after approval
pricing.paid_tiers: Partner pricing based on usage volume; contact sales
pricing.notes: Requires ChargePoint partner agreement and approval. Not self-service — intended for commercial integrations. Fleet programs have different pricing from consumer applications.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: API documentation available to approved partners. Error responses are JSON. Charging session status requires polling — no real-time WebSocket streaming. Webhook delivery for session events.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Station queries are idempotent. Charging session start is not idempotent — duplicate start requests may create duplicate sessions.
known_agent_gotchas: Partner approval required before API access — not self-service
Station availability is near-real-time (30-60 second lag) — not truly real-time
Charging session start requires vehicle to be plugged in — hardware dependency
Network-only: ChargePoint API only covers ChargePoint stations, not other EV networks

security_score_components.security_notes: SOC2 compliant. OAuth2 for user authorization. TLS enforced. Partner vetting process adds security layer. Fleet access controls prevent unauthorized session initiation.
